title: fixed json decoding bug in daemon thread
body: fixed bug in daemon thread that is triggered when trying to convert an empty message_json to dict. Still raising the JSONDecodeError in case decoding fails for any other reason

step_plan.summary: Fix the daemon receiver loop so empty or whitespace-only websocket messages are ignored instead of causing json.loads to raise JSONDecodeError and terminate the thread; still allow JSONDecodeError to propagate for non-empty malformed JSON. Also add the JSONDecodeError import to the module.

steps.operation: Guard against empty websocket messages and handle JSONDecodeError appropriately; add JSONDecodeError import
steps.reason: websocket.recv() can return an empty string or whitespace which causes json.loads('') to raise JSONDecodeError and crash the daemon receiver thread. The receiver should ignore empty/whitespace messages and only allow JSONDecodeError to propagate for genuinely malformed (non-empty) JSON. Also ensure JSONDecodeError is imported so it can be caught explicitly.
steps.side_effects: Prevents the receiver daemon thread from terminating on spurious empty messages, improving stability. Malformed non-empty JSON will still raise JSONDecodeError and surface as before (no silent suppression), so callers/tests that expect that exception will continue to see it. Adding the import has no runtime side effects. Update/add unit/integration tests for the recv loop to cover empty/whitespace messages (should be ignored) and invalid non-empty JSON (should raise). Downstream consumers that relied on the thread crashing for certain error conditions will observe different behavior (thread stays alive), so verify any monitoring or error-handling logic that assumed the previous crash behavior.
